response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software craftsman with ~14 years across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded systems, but the role is a Principal Performance Architect for AI-accelerator hardware. There is no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, deep-learning workload optimization, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD work, or hardware-software co-design. The closest adjacencies (low-latency DLang services, C/C++ at National Instruments, robotic-arm projects) are application-level, not silicon architecture. The educational path is a BS with strong experience, but the experience is misaligned with the core domain. This is a poor fit for a principal-level hardware architecture IC.
strengths: Extensive software engineering experience (14+ years) with strong craftsmanship and testing discipline
Demonstrated low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec in DLang)
Some C/C++ and embedded exposure (LabWindows/CVI, robotic arms, audio synthesis)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling or AI-accelerator hardware architecture experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/SIMD/MIMD background
Profile is application/web-focused, not hardware-software co-design at the silicon level

key_factors.factor: Lack of SoC/IP modeling & accelerator architecture
